channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Tim Walz expects immigration crackdown to end within days with Minnesota in ‘trust but verify mode’
item.description: Walz said he expected to hear more “in the next day or so” about what he said has been an “occupation” and a “retribution campaign” against the state.

item.title: Do you really know what ‘agent’ means? If not, you’re putting your company at risk
item.description: In the first week of February 2026, a social network called Moltbook became the biggest story in AI . Billed as “social media for AI agents,” the Redd...

item.title: Steve Jobs Said This 1 Mindset Is the Difference Between Loving Your Work and Burning Out
item.description: Jobs believed the key to success was playing the long game and loving what you do.

item.title: This designer bootstrapped her way to building a cool-girl fashion brand
item.description: Nili Lotan’s Tribeca flagship has been a fixture in the neighborhood for 20 years. It’s an austere space that brings her aesthetic universe to life, o...

item.title: MrBeast’s business empire stretches far beyond viral YouTube videos
item.description: Jimmy Donaldson might have made his fortune on YouTube, but the man better known as MrBeast has plans for a much wider financial empire—and he’s well ...

item.title: 3 Ways to Identify a Great New Business Idea
item.description: What counts most is securing customers faster than your competitors.

item.title: Google lawyer on YouTube: ‘It’s not social media addiction when it’s not social media and it’s not an addiction’
item.description: Meta lawyer Paul Schmidt spoke of the disagreement within the scientific community over social media addiction.

item.title: We’re in a trade-down economy, and Ulta is winning
item.description: If you’re feeling anxious about the economy, you’re not alone. Consumer confidence is at its lowest in more than a decade . Americans are worried abou...

item.title: This Scene from ‘Breaking Bad’ Provides a Brilliant Lesson for Every Aspiring Entrepreneur
item.description: Because your upside is always limited by your business model.

item.title: Hey Amazon. Justify Your $200B Spend After 30K Layoffs. I Dare You.
item.description: Amazon announces the massive spending spree that all those layoffs allegedly made possible.

item.title: 7 Signs of High-Functioning Burnout, According to Neuroscience
item.description: You can perform at a high level and still be on the edge of collapse, new research warns.

item.title: Powell’s parting gift from the Fed may be more rate cuts than expected, courtesy of deteriorating data
item.description: Recent "releases helped to validate the dovish arguments pushing for more rate cuts this year," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen noted.

item.title: Chappell Roan leaves talent agency after leader Casey Wasserman’s appearance in Epstein files
item.description: Wasserman, the entertainment industry executive, is also the chair of the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

item.title: Compulsive productivity is killing your rest. This is why
item.description: If you ask my friends or colleagues to describe me, the unanimous response would be “she’s someone who gets sh*t done.” It’s become a well-worn badge ...

item.title: Prop bet chaos as Kalshi calls Cardi B’s Super Bowl cameo was ambiguous and Polymarket pays out on disputed wager
item.description: At least one Kalshi trader filed a complaint with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission over how the prediction market handled Sunday.
